exemplar.title: The effect of sustain pedaling on amplitude of piano notes

detailedExemplarData.title: The effect of sustain pedaling on amplitude of piano notes

subject.title: Mathematics Analysis and Approaches (AA)
subject.description:
**IB Mathematics Analysis and Approaches** is a **Group 5** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **number and algebra, functions, geometry and trigonometry, statistics and probability, and calculus**, with a strong focus on **theoretical understanding, formal mathematical reasoning, and analytical problem-solving**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2021**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | Non-calculator written exam | 90 minutes | 120 minutes | 40% |
| **Paper 2** | Calculator allowed written exam | 90 minutes | 120 minutes | 40% |
| **Paper 3** | Calculator allowed problem-solving | N/A | 60 minutes | 20% (HL only) |
| **Mathematical Exploration** | Internal Assessment | 20 hours | 20 hours | 20% |

strengths: Clear, wellâstructured introductionâbodyâconclusion flow with consistent headings, numbered tables and labelled figures
Effective use of cross-references (e.g., âsee appendix Câ) and summary tables to guide the reader
Logical development of arguments step-by-step, making the exploration easy to follow
weaknesses: Minor duplication of content (extended terminology table) adds bulk
Occasional organizational lapses (figures split over pages)
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Although there are small issues of duplication and formatting, the reportâs structure is exceptionally clear and logical.  Sections flow seamlessly, aiding comprehension, and the organisational features (headings, tables, cross-references) elevate the work to the top band for Presentation.

strengths: Consistent and accurate use of mathematical notation for functions, logarithms, regression and half-life calculations
Effective use of multiple representations (tables, graphs, regression outputs) to support the argument
Good logical structure and clear explanatory prose linking each mathematical step
weaknesses: A few minor notation slips (e.g., exponent placement)
Static screenshots limit interactivity of Desmos models
Some assumptions (like fixed phase shift Ï) are introduced without full justification
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The student communicates complex ideas clearly, with mostly consistent notation and well-chosen representations.  Minor lapses do not impede understanding, warranting a band-4 award for Mathematical Communication.

strengths: Strong personal motivation drawn from the studentâs experience as a pianist
Creative adoption of half-life modelling and validation across octaves
Clear evidence of independent thinking in devising bespoke measurement and analysis methods
weaknesses: Mathematical techniques remain largely within standard SL approaches
Approach, while personal, does not reach exceptional innovation
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The exploration displays significant independent thinking and a good level of creativity rooted in personal context.  However, it does not push into the outstanding level of personal engagement required for the top band.

strengths: Meaningful reflection on model limitations (e.g., decay-factor constraints) woven throughout the analysis
Thoughtful evaluation of results via percentage-error validation
Clear suggestions for improvement and consideration of future implications for playing technique
weaknesses: Depth of critical analysis remains surface-level, without probing underlying assumptions
Evaluation, while clear, is not comprehensive or deeply insightful
Limited discussion of measurement uncertainty and alternative models
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The student provides meaningful reflection and thoughtful evaluation of outcomes but stops short of the deep, critical analysis and comprehensive evaluation characteristic of the top band.

strengths: Comprehensive range of mathematical techniques (dBâamplitude conversion, exponential and sinusoidal modeling, regression, half-life calculations)
Well-integrated use of logarithmic linearisation and statistical checks to justify model choices
Clear step-by-step derivations showing thorough understanding of methods
weaknesses: Minor calculation errors in decibel conversion (division by 20 omitted)
Opportunity for more robust exponential regression rather than averaging ratios
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The exploration employs a wide spectrum of SL-level mathematics in a coherent and effective manner.  Minor errors prevent an expertâlevel score, but the overall sophistication and accuracy merit a band-5 award for Use of Mathematics.

strandSelections.reasoning: Explanatory prose accompanies each mathematical step, and most deductions follow logically from preceding statements.  Occasional abrupt assumptions (choice of regression model, fixed phase shift) reduce absolute clarity, placing the strand firmly in the good rather than excellent range.

Calculations are mostly correct and show sound understanding (e.g., correct use of logarithms to linearise).  Nevertheless, some inaccuraciesâsuch as mis-applying the 20 logââ rule when converting dB or assigning metres to dimensionless amplitudesâprevent the award of the top band, but overall understanding remains good and largely accurate.

The student employs tables, graphs, regression outputs and algebraic functions effectively to convey ideas.  Representations are appropriate and support the argument, but interactive elements (e.g., Desmos sliders) are only shown as static screenshots, so the work falls just short of the âhighly effectiveâ criterion.

The student structures the report with clear sections (rationale, methodology, analysis, evaluation, conclusion) that flow in a logical sequence. Arguments are generally developed step-by-step (e.g., data collection â model construction â analysis â validation).  Minor jumps in reasoning occur (such as taking Ï = 50 without explanation), but overall the line of thought can be followed with little difficulty, satisfying the descriptor for a coherent and logically developed exploration rather than the fully polished excellence required for band 4.

Creating decay-plus-sine models, introducing half-life, and validating with percentage error indicate creativity and initiative beyond a routine treatment, yet the mathematics itself remains standard for SL.  This exemplifies a âgood levelâ rather than âexceptionalâ creativity.

The student analyses resultsâidentifying exponential relations, discussing decreasing pedal effect and linking to pianist practiceâwhich shows thoughtfulness. Yet the critique remains surface-level; it lacks the deep, nuanced assessment characteristic of band 3.

Outcomes are evaluated through percentage-error validation and suggestions for improvements (piecewise modelling, better microphones).  Future implications for playing technique are noted.  This is a clear evaluation but not comprehensive enough to reach the highest band.

Reflection is woven through analysis (e.g., recognising decay-factor limitations) and in the evaluation section.  The comments move beyond description to consider effectiveness, giving meaningful reflection, but do not probe assumptions or alternative models in depth; thus band 2 fits best.

Notation for functions, logarithms, half-life, and regression is generally correct and used consistently.  Minor slips (writing 10^{-35.31} instead of 10^{-35.31/20}, unit âmetersâ for amplitude in Audacity) occur but do not hinder comprehension, aligning with âmostly consistentâ use (band 3).

A clear personal voice is evident: the pianistâs frustrations motivate the aim, and reflections on how findings will change pedal use demonstrate ownership.  While personalised, the approach is not especially innovative, placing it in the middle band.

Much of the content is relevant to the stated aim, but extended definitional tables, lengthy screen-captures and repeatedly listing every numerical step add bulk without advancing the mathematics.  These unnecessary elements compromise conciseness; hence the work is âmostly relevant with some unnecessary elements,â matching band 2.

A substantial range of mathematics is employed: dBâamplitude conversion, sinusoidal modelling, exponential decay, logarithmic linearisation, regression, half-life calculations, percentage-error validation and extrapolation.  These elements are well integrated, demonstrating comprehensive use rather than merely appropriate or expert-level sophistication.

The student selects the topic from personal experience and devises bespoke methods (half-life approach, validation across octaves). These choices show significant independent thinking, though not at the âoutstandingâ level since many modelling decisions follow textbook approaches.

Headings, numbered tables and labelled figures give the investigation a recognisable introductionâbodyâconclusion structure. Cross-references (e.g., âsee appendix Câ) and summary tables help the reader navigate.  Some duplication (long terminology table, repeated screenshots) and occasional organisational lapses (figures split over pages) prevent the piece from reaching the âexceptionally well organisedâ standard of band 4.

Trigonometric and exponential modelling, logarithmic transformations and statistical checks all lie comfortably within the SL syllabus and are fully utilised.  The work is clearly at the expected level and applied in context; however, it does not extend to non-syllabus content that would justify band 5 or 6.

annotations.comment: Align the Desmos and Audacity plots on identical axis scales to improve direct visual comparison of amplitude variations.
Focusing on decay factors at a single time point (t=1) is insufficient; the sustaining effect should be analyzed as a function of t.
Consider deriving a general expression for amplitude ratio over time to characterize the pedal effect continuously rather than at fixed t.
Annotate the regression equations and RÂ² values directly on the trendline graphs to make the mathematical justification explicit.
The log-log interpretation of trendline equations is clear and demonstrates strong logical clarity in mathematical communication.
The table of initial amplitudes lacks discussion of measurement uncertainty; include error margins to reflect amplitude variability.
The generalization section neatly consolidates findings against the hypothesis, showing coherent logical development.
The conclusion synthesizes results but fails to discuss broader implications or potential alternative models beyond exponential.
Reflect in the conclusion on the challenge of defining piecewise behavior and what was learned about function fitting.
The student effectively connects personal experience as a pianist to the rationale, demonstrating clear motivation and personalized context for the investigation.
The hypothesis that the pedal effect is independent of note lacks justification; the student should reference prior studies or pilot data to support this claim.
Consider adding units and scale information when describing wave amplitudes in the text accompanying Figure 1 to avoid confusion between meters and relative units.
The extensive terminology table adds unnecessary bulk and distracts from the main mathematical narrative; consider consolidating or moving definitions to an appendix.
The use of photographic and tabular representations effectively supports comprehension, providing multiple entry points for readers to understand piano mechanics.
The definition of âdecibelâ in the table is too superficial; include its logarithmic relation to intensity to strengthen technical accuracy.
The methodology section clearly outlines the use of Audacity for data collection and conversion of decibels to amplitude, establishing transparency in procedure.
The formula $$\mathrm{Amplitude}=10^{\frac{\text{initial dB}}{20}}$$ is stated correctly, but the application in examples should explicitly show the division by 20.
When presenting the âAmplifyâ tool screenshot, annotate how you infer the negative dB value from the positive amplification reading to improve clarity.
A calculation error occurs in converting dB: the expression $$10^{-35.31}$$ should read $$10^{-35.31/20}$$, leading to an incorrect amplitude value.
Explicitly demonstrate the substitution of -35.31 dB into $$A=10^{dB/20}$$ with numeric steps to improve mathematical transparency.
The Desmos scatter plot in Figure 8 effectively illustrates data correlation before model fitting, supporting the modeling choice.
Justification of the sinusoidal model with an RÂ² value of 0.7129 is appropriate and shows deliberate model selection.
The introduction of phase shift Ï is not accompanied by a method; explain how you determined Ï=50 from the data.
Reflect on the impact of excluding negative amplitude values on the modelâs fidelity to the actual signal decay.
The step-by-step derivation of individual decay ratios and averaging to find the decay factor is clearly presented.
Averaging discrete decay factors is simplistic; fitting an exponential regression on the full log-linear dataset would yield a more robust decay constant.
Consider performing an exponential regression on all amplitude-time pairs to determine the decay factor k instead of averaging ratios.
The arbitrary assignment of phase shift Ï=50 requires justification; specify how seconds were converted to phase units.
The evaluation of model vs. real signal in figures 10â11 provides insightful reflections on model limitations.
Applying the half-life concept normalizes decay analysis and is a clever way to compare sustaining effects independent of time.
The half-life is read from Desmos without error estimate; discuss possible uncertainties introduced by reading the graph.
Regression plots in Figures 15â16 are well executed, but discussion of outliers or leverage points would strengthen the analysis.
The references list shows varied sources but lacks consistent formatting; adopt a single citation style for clarity.
Standardize all bibliography entries (e.g., APA format) and ensure each citation includes author, date, title, and accessible URL.
